author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XXIV. 32, 33.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Now learn a parable of the fig tree; when his branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is nigh: so likewise ye, when ye shall see all these things, know that it is near, even at the doors.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Forasmuch as He had said, “Immediately after the tribulation of those days;” but they sought of this, after how long a time it should be, and desired to know in particular the very day, therefore He puts also the similitude of the fig tree, indicating that the interval was not great, but that in quick succession would occur His advent also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He declared not by the parable alone, but by the words that follow, saying, “know that it is near, even at the doors.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whereby He foretells another thing also, a spiritual summer, and a calm that should be on that day (after the present tempest) for the righteous; but to the sinners the contrary, winter after summer, which He declares in what follows, saying, that the day shall come upon them, when they are living in luxury.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not for this intent only did He put forward this about the fig tree, in order to declare the interval; for it was possible to have set this before them in other ways as well; but that he might hereby also confirm His saying, as assuredly thus to come to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as this of the fig tree is of necessity, so that too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thus, wherever He is minded to speak of that which will assuredly come to pass, He brings forward the necessary courses of nature, both Himself, and the blessed Paul imitating Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also when speaking of His resurrection, He saith, “When the corn of wheat hath fallen into the earth, except it die, it abideth alone; but if it die, it bringeth forth much fruit.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whereby also the blessed Paul being instructed uses the same similitude,“Thou fool,” he saith, “that which thou sowest is not quickened, except it die.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this, that they might not straightway return to it again, and say, “When?” he brings to their remembrance the things that had been said, saying, “Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass, till all these things be fulfilled!”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: All these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I pray thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Those about Jerusalem, those about the wars, about the famines, about the pestilences, about the earthquakes, about the false Christs, about the false prophets, about the sowing of the gospel everywhere, the seditions, the tumults, all the other things, which we said were to occur until His coming.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then, one may ask, did He say, “This generation?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Speaking not of the generation then living, but of that of the believers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He is wont to distinguish a generation not by times only, but also by the mode of religious service, and practice; as when He saith, “This is the generation of them that seek the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what He said above, “All these must come to pass,”and again, “the gospel shall be preached,”this He declares here also, saying, All these things shall surely come to pass, and the generation of the faithful shall remain, cut off by none of the things that have been mentioned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For both Jerusalem shall perish, and the more part of the Jews shall be destroyed, but over this generation shall nothing prevail, not famine, not pestilence, not earthquake, nor the tumults of wars, not false Christs, not false prophets, not deceivers, not traitors, not those that cause to offend, not the false brethren, nor any other such like temptation whatever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then to lead them on more in faith, He saith, “Heaven and earth shall pass away, but my words shall not pass away;”that is, it were more easy for these firm, fixed, and immoveable bodies to be blotted out, than for ought of my words to fall to the ground.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he who gainsays these things, let him test His sayings, and when he hath found them true (for so he surely will find them) from what is past, let him believe also the things to come, and let him search out all things with diligence, and he will see the actual events bearing witness to the truth of the prophecy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the elements He hath brought forward, at once to declare, that the church is of more honor than Heaven and earth, and at the same time to indicate Himself by this also to be maker of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since He was speaking of the end, a thing disbelieved by many, He brought forward Heaven and earth, indicating His unspeakable power, and showing with great authority, that He is Lord of all, and by these things rendering His sayings deserving of credit, even with those who are much given to doubt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But of that day and hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels of Heaven,neither the Son,but the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By saying, not the angels, He stopped their mouths, that they should not seek to learn what these angels know not; and by saying, “neither the Son,” forbids them not only to learn, but even to inquire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in proof that therefore He said this, see after His resurrection, when He saw they were become over curious, how He stopped their mouths more decidedly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For now indeed He hath mentioned infallible signs, many and endless; but then He saith merely, “It is not for you to know times or seasons.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And then that they might not say, we are driven to perplexity, we are utterly scorned, we are not held worthy so much as of this, He says, “which the Father hath put in His own power.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this, because He was exceedingly careful to honor them, and to conceal nothing from them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He refers it to His Father, both to make the thing awful, and to exclude that of which He had spoken from their inquiry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since if it be not this, but He is ignorant of it, when will He know it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Will it be together with us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But who would say this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the Father He knoweth clearly, even as clearly as He knoweth the Son; and of the day is He ignorant?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Moreover, “the Spirit indeed searcheth even the deep things of God,”and doth not He know so much as the time of the judgment?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But how He ought to judge He knoweth, and of the secrets of each He hath a full perception; and what is far more common than that, of this could He be ignorant?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how, if “all things were made by Him, and without Him was not even one thing made,”was He ignorant of the day?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He who made the worlds,it is quite plain that He made the times also; and if the times, even that day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then is He ignorant of that which He made?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And ye indeed say that ye know even His substance,but that the Son not even the day, the Son, who is always in the bosom of the Father; and yet His substance is much greater than the days, even infinitely greater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then, while assigning to yourselves the greater things, do you not allow even the less to the Son, “in whom are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But neither do you know what God is in His substance, though ten thousand times ye talk thus madly, neither is the Son ignorant of the day, but is even in full certainty thereof.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause, I say, when He had told all things, both the times and the seasons, and had brought it to the very doors (“for it is near,” He saith, “even at the doors”), He was silent as to the day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou seek after the day and hour, thou shalt not hear them of me, saith He; but if of times and preludes, without hiding anything, I will tell thee all exactly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that indeed I am not ignorant of it, I have shown by many things; having mentioned intervals, and all the things that are to occur, and how short from this present time until the day itself (for this did the parable of the fig tree indicate), and I lead thee to the very vestibule; and if I do not open unto thee the doors, this also I do for your good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that thou mayest learn by another thing also, that the silence is not a mark of ignorance on His part, see, together with what we have mentioned, how He sets forth another sign also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But as in the days of Noe they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that the flood came, and took all away; so shall also the coming of the Son of Man be.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things He spake, showing that He should come on a sudden, and unexpectedly, and when the more part were living luxuriously.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For Paul too saith this, writing on this wise, “When they shall speak of peace and safety, then sudden destruction cometh upon them;” and to show how unexpected, He said, “as travail upon a woman with child.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then doth He say, “after the tribulation of those days?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if there be luxury then, and peace, and safety, as Paul saith, how doth He say, “after the tribulation of those days?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If there be luxury, how is there tribulation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Luxury for them that are in a state of insensibility and peace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He said not, when there is peace, but “when they speak of peace and safety,” indicating their insensibility to be such as of those in Noah’s time, for that amid such evils they lived in luxury.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not so the righteous, but they were passing their time in tribulation and dejection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whereby He shows, that when Antichrist is come, the pursuit of unlawful pleasures shall be more eager among the transgressors, and those that have learnt to despair of their own salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then shall be gluttony, then revellings, and drunkenness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also most of all He puts forth an example corresponding to the thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as when the ark was making, they believed not, saith He; but while it was set in the midst of them, proclaiming beforehand the evils that are to come, they, when they saw it, lived in pleasure, just as though nothing dreadful were about to take place; so also now, Antichrist indeed shall appear, after whom is the end, and the punishments at the end, and vengeance intolerable; but they that are held by the intoxication of wickedness shall not so much as perceive the dreadful nature of the things that are on the point of being done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also Paul saith, “as travail upon a woman with child,” even so shall those fearful and incurable evils come upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore did He not speak of the ills in Sodom?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was His will to introduce an example embracing all men,and disbelieved after it was foretold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So therefore, as by the more part the things to come are disbelieved, He confirms those things by the past, terrifying their minds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And together with the points I have mentioned, He shows this also, that of the former things also He was the doer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then again He sets another sign, by all which things He makes it evident, that He is not ignorant of the day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is the sign?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then shall two be in the field; one shall be taken, and one left.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Two women shall be grinding at the mill, one shall be taken, and one left.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Watch therefore, for ye know not what hour your Lord doth come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And all these things are both proofs that He knew, and calculated to turn them from their inquiry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So for this cause He spake also of the days of Noe, for this cause He said too, “Two shall be on the bed,” signifying this, that He should come upon them thus unexpectedly, when they were thus without thought, and “two women grinding at the mill,” which also of itself is not the employment of them that are taking thought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And together with this, He declares that as well servants as masters should be both taken and left, both those who are at ease, and those in toil, as well from the one rank as from the other; even as in the Old Testament He saith, “From him that sitteth upon the throne to the captive woman that is at the mill.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since He had said, that hardly are the rich saved, He shows that not even these are altogether lost, neither are the poor saved all of them, but both out of these and out of those are men saved, and lost.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to me He seems to declare, that at night will be the advent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this Luke too saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how accurately He knows all things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this again, that they may not ask about it, He added, “Watch therefore, for ye know not what hour your Lord doth come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He said not, “I know not,” but, “ye know not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when He had brought them well nigh to the very hour, and had placed them there, again He deters them from the inquiry, from a desire that they should be striving always.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He saith, “Watch,” showing that for the sake of this, He did not tell it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But know this, that if the good man of the house had known in what watch the thief would come, he would have watched, and would not have suffered his house to be broken up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore be ye also ready, for in such an hour as ye think not the Son of Man cometh.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this intent He tells them not, in order that they may watch, that they may be always ready; therefore He saith, When ye look not for it, then He will come, desiring that they should be anxiously waiting, and continually in virtuous action.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But His meaning is like this: if the common sort of men knew when they were to die, they would surely strive earnestly at that hour.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In order therefore that they may strive, not at that hour only, therefore He tells them not either the common hour, or the hour of each, desiring them to be ever looking for this, that they may be always striving.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He made the end of each man’s life also uncertain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this, He openly calls Himself Lord, having nowhere spoken so distinctly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But here He seems to me also to put to shame the careless, that not even as much care as they that expect a thief have taken for their money, not even this much do these take for their own soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they indeed, when they expect it, watch, and suffer none of the things in their house to be carried off; but ye, although knowing that He will come, and come assuredly, continue not watching, saith He, and ready so as not to be carried away hence unprepared.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that the day cometh unto destruction for them that sleep.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as that man, if he had known, would have escaped, so also ye, if ye be ready, escape free.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, as He had fallen upon the mention of the judgment, He directs His discourse to the teachers next, speaking of punishment and honors; and having put first them that do right, He ends with them that continue in sin, making His discourse to close with that which is alarming.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He first saith this, “Who then is the faithful and wise servant, whom his Lord shall set over His household to give them their meat in theirdue season?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Blessed is that servant, whom his Lord when He cometh shall find so doing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Verily I say unto you, that He shall make him ruler over all His goods.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Tell me, is this too the language of one who is in ignorance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if because He said, “neither doth the Son know,” thou sayest He is ignorant of it; as He saith, “who then?” what wilt thou say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wilt thou say He is ignorant of this too?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Away with the thought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not even one of them that are frantic would say this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet in the former case one might assign a cause; but here not even this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what when He said, “Peter, lovest thou me?”asking it, knew He not so much as this? nor when He said, “Where have ye laid him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the Father too will be found to be saying such things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He Himself likewise saith, “Adam, where art thou?”and, “The cry of Sodom and Gomorrah is waxed great before me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I will go down therefore, and see whether their doings be according to their cry which cometh unto me, and if not, I will know.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And elsewhere He saith, “Whether they will hear, whether they will understand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in the gospel too, “It may be they will reverence my Son:”all which are expressions of ignorance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not in ignorance did He say these things, but as compassing objects such as became Him: in the case of Adam, that He might drive him to make an excuse for his sin: in that of the Sodomites, that He might teach us never to be positive, till we are present at the very deeds; in that of the prophet, that the prediction might not appear in the judgment of the foolish a kind of compulsion to disobedience; and in the parable in the gospel, that He might show that they ought to have done this, and to have reverenced the Son: but here, as well that they may not be curious, nor over busy again, as that He might indicate that this was a rare and precious thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see of what great ignorance this saying is indicative, if at least He know not even him that is set over.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He blesses him indeed, “For blessed,” saith He, “is that servant;” but He saith not who this is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For who is he,” He saith, “whom His Lord shall set over?” and, “Blessed is he whom He shall find so doing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But these things are spoken not of money only, but also of speech, and of power, and of gifts, and of every stewardship, wherewith each is entrusted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This parable would suit rulers in the state also, for every one is bound to make full use of what he hath for the common advantage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If it be wisdom thou hast, if power, if wealth, if what it may, let it not be for the hurt of thy fellow-servants, neither for thine own ruin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause, therefore, He requires both things of him, wisdom, and fidelity: for sin arises from folly also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He calls him faithful then, because he hath purloined nothing, neither misspent his Lord’s goods without aim or fruit; and wise, because he knew how to dispense the things given him, according as was fit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed we have need of both things, as well not to purloin the goods of our Master, as also to dispense them as is fit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if the one be wanting, the other halteth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if he be faithful and steal not, yet were to waste and to spend upon that which concerned him not, great were the blame; and if he should know how to dispense it well, yet were to purloin, again there is no common charge against him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And let us also that have money listen to these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not unto teachers only doth He discourse, but also unto the rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For either sort were entrusted with riches; those that teach with the more necessary wealth, ye with what is inferior.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When then at the time that the teachers are scattering abroad the greater, ye are not willing to show forth your liberality even in the less, or rather not liberality but honesty (for ye give the things of another), what excuse will you have?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now, before the punishment of them that do the contrary things, let us hear the honor of him that approveth himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For verily I say unto you, He will set him over all His goods.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What can be equal to this honor? what manner of speech will be able to set forth the dignity, the blessedness, when the King of Heaven, He that possesseth all things, is about to set a man over “all His goods?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He calleth him wise, because he knew, not to give up great things for small, but having been temperate here, hath attained to Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this, as He ever doth, not by the honor only laid up for the good, but also by the punishment threatened against the wicked, doth He correct the hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He added, “But and if the evil servant say in his heart, my Lord delayeth His coming; and shall begin to smite his fellow servants, and shall eat and drink with the drunken: the Lord of that servant shall come in a day when he looketh not for Him, and in an hour that he is not aware of,and shall cut him asunder,and shall appoint him his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if any one should say, Seest thou what a thought hath entered into his mind, because of the day’s not being known, “my Lord,” he saith, “delayeth His coming?” we should affirm, that it was not because the day is not known, but because the servant is evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Else wherefore came not this thought into the heart of the faithful and wise servant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what, even though the Lord tarry, O wretched man, surely thou lookest that He will come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why then dost thou not take care?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hence then we learn, that He doth not so much as tarry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this judgment is not the Lord’s, but that of the evil servant’s mind, wherefore also he is blamed for this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in proof that He doth not tarry, hear Paul saying, “The Lord is at hand, be careful for nothing;”and, “He that cometh will come, and will not tarry.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But do thou hear also what followeth, and learn how continually He reminds them of their ignorance of the day, showing that this is profitable to the servants, and fitted to waken and thoroughly to rouse them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what though some gained nothing hereby?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither by other things profitable for them were some profited, but nevertheless He ceaseth not to do His part.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is the purport of that which followeth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For He shall come in a day when he looketh not for Him, and in an hour that he is not aware of;”and shall inflict upon him extreme punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how even everywhere He puts this, the fact of their ignorance, indicating that it was profitable, and by this making them always earnest minded?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is the point at which He labors, that we should be always on the watch; and since it is always in luxury that we are supine, but in afflictions we are braced up, therefore everywhere He saith this, that when there is relaxation, then come the terrors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as further back He showed this by the example of Noah, even so here He saith it is, when that servant is drunken, when he is beating, and that his punishment shall be intolerable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let us not regard only the punishment appointed for him, but let us look to this other point too, lest we ourselves also be un awares to ourselves doing the same things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to this servant are they like, who have money, and give not to the needy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thou too art steward of thine own possessions, not less than he who dispenses the alms of the church.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As then he has not a right to squander at random and at hazard the things given by you for the poor, since they were given for the maintenance of the poor; even so neither mayest thou squander thine own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even though thou hast received an inheritance from thy father, and hast in this way all thou possessest: even thus all are God’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And then thou for thy part desirest that what thou hast given should be thus carefully dispensed, and thinkest thou not that God will require His own of us with greater strictness, or that He suffers them to be wasted at random?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These things are not, they are not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because for this end, He left these things in thine hand, in order “to give them their meat in due season.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what meaneth, “in due season?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To the needy, to the hungry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as thou gavest to thy fellow-servant to dispense, even so doth the Lord will thee too to spend these things on what is needful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore though He was able to take them away from thee, He left them, that thou mightest have opportunity to show forth virtue; that bringing us into need one of another, He might make our love for one another more fervent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thou, when thou hast received, so far from giving, dost even beat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet if not to give be blame, what excuse is there for beating?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this, it seems to me, He speaks, hinting at the insolent, and the covetous, and indicating the charge to be heavy, when they beat them, whom they were commanded to feed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He seemeth to be here hinting also at those that live in luxury, since for luxury too there is laid up a great punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For He eateth and drinketh,” it is said, “with the drunken,” pointing at gluttony.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not for this purpose didst thou receive, that thou should spend it on luxury, but that thou shouldest lay it out on alms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What! are they thine own things which thou hast?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With the goods of the poor hast thou been entrusted, though thou be possessed of them by honest labor, or though it be by inheritance from thy father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What, could not God have taken away these things from thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He doth not this, to give thee power to be liberal to the poor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark thou, I pray thee, how throughout all the parables He punishes them that lay not out their money upon the needy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither had the virgins robbed other men’s goods, but they had not given their own; neither had he that buried the one talent embezzled, but he had not doubled; neither are they that overlooked the hungry punished, because they seized the possessions of others, but because they did not lay out their own, like as also this servant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us hearken, as many as please the belly, as many as lay out on costly banquets the riches that pertain not at all to us, but belong to the needy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For do not, because out of great love to man thou art commanded to give as of thine, therefore suppose these things to be indeed thine own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He lent them to thee, that thou mightest be able to approve thyself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do not then suppose them to be thine, when giving Him His own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither, if thou hadst lent to any one, that he might go and be able to find means of gain, wouldest thou say the money was his.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To thee then also hath God given, that thou mightest traffic for Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Make not then the exceeding greatness of His love to man a cause of ingratitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Consider of what prayer it were a worthy object, to be able to find after baptism a way to do away one’s sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If He had not said this, Give alms, how many would have said, Would it were possible to give money, and so be freed from the ills to come!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But since this hath become possible, again are they become supine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But I give,” thou sayest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou hast not yet given as much as she, who cast in the two mites; or rather not so much as the half, nor a very small part of what she gave, but thou layest out the greater part on useless expenses, on banquets, and drunkenness, and extreme extravagance; now bidding, now bidden; now spending, now constraining others to spend; so that the punishment is even rendered twofold for thee, both from what thyself doest, and what thou movest others to do.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See at any rate how He Himself blames His servant for this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For he eateth,” He saith, “and drinketh with the drunken.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not the drunken only, but those that are with them, doth He punish, and very fitly, because (together with corrupting their own selves) they make light also of the salvation of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But nothing does so much provoke God, as for us to be inclined to overlook the things that concern our neighbor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore showing His anger, He commands him to be cut asunder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He also affirmed love to be a distinguishing mark of His disciples, since it is altogether necessary that he who loveth should take thought for the things of his beloved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To this way then let us hold, for this is especially the way that leads up to Heaven, which renders men followers of Christ, which makes them, as far as possible, like God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See at any rate how these virtues are more needful, which have their dwelling by this way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And, if ye will, let us make an inquiry into them, and let us bring forth the sentences from the judgment of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let there be then two ways of most holy life, and let the one secure the goodness of him that practises it, but the other of his neighbor also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us see whether is the more approved and leads us to the summit of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Surely he, who seeks his own things only, will receive even from Paul endless blame, and when I say from Paul, I mean from Christ, but the other commendations and crowns.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence is this evident?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear what His language is to one, what to the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Let no man seek his own, but every man another’s wealth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou he rejects the one, and brings in the other?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, “Let every one of you please his neighbor for good to edification.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then comes also the praise beyond words with an admonition, “For even Christ pleased not Himself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even these judgments then are sufficient to show the victory; but that this may be done even superabundantly, let us see amongst good works, which are confined to ourselves, and which pass over from us to others also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Fasting then, and lying on the bare ground, and keeping virginity, and a self-denying life, these things bring their advantage to the persons themselves who do them; but those that pass from ourselves to our neighbors are almsgiving, teaching, charity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear then Paul in this matter also saying, “Though I bestow all my goods to feed the poor, and though I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, I am nothing profited.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou it in itself gloriously celebrated, and crowned?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if ye be willing, from a third point also let us compare them; and let the one fast, and deny himself, and be a martyr, and be burnt to death, but let another delay his martyrdom for his neighbor’s edification; and let him not only delay it, but let him even depart without martyrdom; who will be the more approved after his removal hence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: We need not have many words, nor a long circumlocution.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the blessed Paul is at hand, giving his judgment, and saying, “To depart and to be with Christ is better, nevertheless to abide in the flesh is more needful for you;”even to his removal unto Christ did he prefer his neighbor’s edification.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is in the highest sense to be with Christ, even to be doing His will, but nothing is so much His will, as that which is for one’s neighbor’s good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wilt thou that I tell thee a fourth proof also of these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Peter, lovest thou me,” saith He; “Feed my sheep:”and having asked him a third time, declared this to be an infallible proof of love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not to priests only is this said, but to every one of us also, who are also entrusted with a little flock.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For do not despise it, because it is a little flock: For “my Father,” He saith, “hath pleasure in them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Each of us hath a sheep, let him lead that to the proper pastures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And let the man, as soon as he has risen from his bed, seek after nothing else, but how he may do and say something whereby he may render his whole house more reverent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The woman again, let her be indeed a good housekeeper; but before attending to this, let her have another more needful care, that the whole household may work the works of Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if in worldly matters, before attending to the affairs of our household, we labor diligently to pay public dues, that we may not for our undutifulness in these matters be beaten and dragged to the market places, and suffer ten thousand unseemly things; much more ought we to do this in things spiritual, and to render what is due to God, the King of all, first, that we may not come to that place, “where is gnashing of teeth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And after these virtues let us seek, which together with our own salvation will be able in the greatest degree to profit our neighbor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such is almsgiving, such is prayer, or rather even this latter is by the former made efficacious, and furnished with wings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For thy prayers,” it is said, “and thine alms are come up for a memorial before God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not prayers only, but fasting also hath its strength from hence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Shouldest thou fast without almsgiving; the act is not so much as counted for fasting; but such a one is worse than a gluttonous man and a drunkard; and so much worse, as cruelty is a more grievous thing than luxury.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why do I speak of fasting?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Though thou practise self-denial, though thou practise virginity, thou art set without the bridechamber, if thou hast not almsgiving.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet what is equal to virginity, which not even in the new dispensation hath come under the compulsion of law, on account of its high excellence? but nevertheless it is cast out, when it hath not almsgiving.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if virgins are cast out, because they have not this in due abundance, who will be able without this to obtain pardon?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is no man, but he must quite of necessity perish, who hath not this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, if in worldly matters no man lives for himself, but artisan, and soldier, and husbandman, and merchant, all of them contribute to the common good, and to their neighbor’s advantage; much more ought we to do this in things spiritual.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is most properly to live: since he at least who is living for himself only, and overlooking all others, is useless, and is not so much as a human being, nor of our race.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then, thou wouldest say, if I neglect my own interests, while seeking after the good of the rest?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is not possible, for one who seeks after the good of the rest to overlook his own; for he who seeks after the good of the rest pains no man, but pities all, helps them to the utmost of his powers; will rob no man, will covet the goods of no man, will not steal, will not bear false witness; will abstain from all wickedness, will apply himself to all virtue, and will pray for his enemies, and do good to them that plot against him, and will neither revile any, nor speak ill of them, though he hear from them ten thousand evil things; but will speak the words of the apostle: “Who is weak, and I am not weak? who is offended, and I burn not?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when looking to our own good, it is not quite sure that the good of the rest will follow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By all which things being persuaded that it is not possible for one to be saved, who hath not looked to the common good, and seeing this man that was cut asunder, and him that buried his talent, let us choose this way, that we may also attain unto eternal life, unto which God grant we may all attain, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory, world without end.
